local_names.romanized_local: Sōng lù shuǐ jīng jūn gū jiǎo

aliases: Sōng lù shuǐ jīng jūn gū jiǎo
Mushroom with Truffle and Vegetables Dumplings

english: Mushroom with Truffle and Vegetables Dumplings
chinese: Sōng lù shuǐ jīng jūn gū jiǎo
romanized: Sōng lù shuǐ jīng jūn gū jiǎo

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for crystal-skin mushroom dumplings with truffle. FOODON_03315615 (dumpling food product) is the closest parent class. A more specific term for har gow-style crystal dumplings is not available in the current FoodOn release.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers filled pasta/dumplings broadly. No specific code exists for crystal-skin wheat-starch dumplings with mushroom and truffle filling in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps to this specific dim sum preparation. SNOMED CT does not maintain granular codes for individual dim sum dish types.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 3 pieces of crystal mushroom dumpling, weighing approximately 90 g, as typically served in a dim sum basket.

nutrient_source.title: Truffle Crystal Mushroom Dumpling — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Truffle Crystal Mushroom Dumpling (cr_id G00010689). All core macros and micronutrients are taken verbatim from the HPB record at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and dietary fibre are not reported in the HPB entry and are estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous entries for steamed wheat-starch dumplings with vegetable filling (USDA SR Legacy item 'Dumpling, steamed, vegetable filling').

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on steamed wheat-starch dumpling (har gow style)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for truffle crystal mushroom dumplings. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on the closest analogous food: steamed wheat-starch dumplings (har gow / crystal skin dumplings) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. The crystal skin is made from wheat starch and tapioca starch, which have a moderate GI profile when steamed. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (55) × carbohydrate per serving (8.03 g/100g × 90 g serving = 7.2 g) / 100 = 4.0 GL units.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium at 46 mg/100g with modest potassium at 82 mg/100g
hypertension.guidance: Truffle crystal mushroom dumplings are suitable for individuals with hypertension given their very low sodium content (46 mg/100g); however, patients should avoid high-sodium dipping sauces such as soy sauce or chilli sauce, which can substantially increase total sodium intake per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (8.03 g/100g), negligible sugar (0.21 g/100g), moderate GI (~55), and meaningful protein (8.71 g/100g) supporting glycaemic moderation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Truffle crystal mushroom dumplings have a low carbohydrate load and a low estimated GI of approximately 55; individuals with type 2 diabetes may consume a standard serving of 3 pieces (approximately 90 g, ~7.2 g carbohydrate, GL approximately 4) without significant glycaemic impact, provided refined-starch dipping sauces and high-sugar condiments are avoided.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat (3.78 g/100g), low saturated fat (~0.9 g/100g), low sodium, and mushroom-derived beta-glucans with cardioprotective properties
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Truffle crystal mushroom dumplings present a low cardiovascular risk profile due to their low total fat, low saturated fat, and very low sodium content; the mushroom filling contributes beta-glucans that may support LDL cholesterol reduction, making this dish appropriate for heart-healthy dietary patterns when consumed without high-fat or high-sodium accompaniments.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Mushroom beta-glucans with immune-modulating anti-inflammatory properties; low saturated fat; absence of trans fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Mushrooms in the filling provide beta-glucans with immune-modulating and anti-inflammatory properties; truffle and sesame oil contribute polyphenols, and the absence of trans fat and low saturated fat content make this dish compatible with an anti-inflammatory dietary approach for rheumatoid arthritis management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Truffle Crystal Mushroom Dumpling is a premium dim sum dish originating from Chinese culinary tradition and widely served in Singapore's Chinese restaurants and dim sum establishments. Each dumpling features a translucent crystal skin made from wheat starch and tapioca starch, which becomes semi-transparent and slightly chewy when steamed, revealing the filling within. The filling consists of finely chopped mushrooms — typically shiitake or oyster mushrooms — combined with aromatic truffle (or truffle-infused oil), mixed vegetables, and seasonings. The dumplings are steamed to order and served in portions of three pieces per basket. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides 101 kcal, 8.71 g protein, 3.78 g fat, and 8.03 g carbohydrate with only 0.21 g sugar and 46 mg sodium, making it one of the lower-calorie and lower-sodium dim sum options. The moderate estimated glycemic index of approximately 55 and low glycemic load per serving (~4 GL units) make it suitable for individuals managing blood glucose. The mushroom filling contributes iron (1.79 mg/100g), potassium (82 mg/100g), and beta-glucans with potential cardioprotective and immune-modulating effects. Clinically, this dish is appropriate for individuals with hypertension, type 2 diabetes, and cardiovascular disease when consumed without high-sodium dipping sauces. It is vegetarian-friendly and halal-compatible, and is commonly served at lunch or dinner as part of a shared dim sum spread.
common_user_queries: Is truffle crystal mushroom dumpling healthy?
How many calories in crystal mushroom dumpling?
Is crystal mushroom dumpling suitable for diabetics?
What is the sodium content of truffle dumpling?
What is the GI of crystal skin dumpling?
How much protein in mushroom dumpling?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010689) at a stated confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat and dietary fibre are estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogous entries for steamed vegetable dumplings and documented accordingly. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for wheat-starch steamed dumplings. All sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (0.9 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for steamed vegetable dumplings (wheat-starch wrapper), as the HPB record does not report saturated fat separately. 2. Dietary fibre (1.2 g/100g) is estimated from the same USDA SR Legacy analogous entry, as the HPB record does not report dietary fibre. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this specific dim sum preparation is not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 4. trans_fat_g is set to 0.0 (confirmed negligible/absent for steamed wheat-starch dumplings with vegetable filling; no added hydrogenated fats in standard preparation). 5. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for this specific preparation is not reported in the HPB database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 6. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are set to -1: detailed vitamin and trace mineral data for this specific dim sum preparation are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 7. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on steamed wheat-starch dumplings (har gow style) from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for truffle crystal mushroom dumplings has been published. 8. Truffle oil used in commercial preparations may be synthetic (2,4-dithiapentane-based) rather than derived from real truffle; this does not affect macronutrient values but may affect polyphenol content claims. 9. is_vegan set to false due to the presence of cholesterol (28 mg/100g) in the HPB record, suggesting possible egg or animal-derived ingredient in the filling or wrapper; is_vegetarian set to true as no meat is indicated. 10. ontology.foodon_match_type set to 'closest_parent' and ontology.foodex2_match_type set to 'closest_parent' as these are broader-class matches; the allowed values per validator are 'closest_parent', 'exact', or 'none'.
